api/spec/packages/aip/src/currencies.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the billing currencies API: fiat and custom currency models plus list/create operations. Compiles to v3 OpenAPI spec and SDKs; no hand-written Go or OpenAPI here.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none
patterns.description: Currency is a @discriminated union with envelope:'none' and discriminatorPropertyName:'type'. New currency variants must follow this pattern.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Currency { fiat: CurrencyFiat, custom: CurrencyCustom }

patterns.name: Generic base model with CurrencyType parameter
patterns.description: CurrencyBase<T extends CurrencyType> holds common fields. Type-specific models spread CurrencyBase with the concrete type and add their own fields.

patterns.name: @visibility on every field
patterns.description: All fields must carry explicit @visibility(Lifecycle.*) annotations. Read-only system fields use Lifecycle.Read only; create+read fields use both.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model, enum, scalar
patterns.description: Every exported model, enum, and scalar must have @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") to stabilize generated SDK type names.

patterns.name: Three stability @extension decorators on every operation
patterns.description: Every operation must carry @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true), @extension(Shared.PrivateExtension, true), @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true) for API maturity tracking.

patterns.name: PagePaginationQuery spread + deepObject filter
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery and pass filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCurrenciesParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in currency.tsp, HTTP operations in operations.tsp
patterns.description: Model definitions live in currency.tsp (no HTTP imports). HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) live only in operations.tsp which imports @typespec/http.
patterns.example: currency.tsp: model CurrencyCustom { ... } | operations.tsp: interface CurrenciesCustomOperations { @post create(...) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines CurrencyType enum, Currency union, CurrencyBase generic, CurrencyFiat, CurrencyCustom, and CurrencyCodeCustom scalar. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding @get/@post or importing @typespec/http here breaks separation of concerns. Missing @friendlyName causes unstable SDK names.

key_file_guides.role: Declares CurrenciesOperations (list) and CurrenciesCustomOperations (create) interfaces with HTTP decorators, stability extensions, and filter model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting stability @extension decorators breaks internal API maturity tracking. Using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery causes drift.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point that imports currency.tsp, operations.tsp, and cost-bases/operations.tsp in order.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files in this folder must be imported here; otherwise they are silently excluded from compilation.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`
Declaring fields without @visibility — fields default to all lifecycle phases, leaking write-only or system fields into create payloads
Adding operations without the three @extension stability decorators — breaks internal API maturity tracking
Using a @friendlyName that duplicates an existing model name — causes SDK type collisions
Importing @typespec/http in currency.tsp (model file) — HTTP decorators belong only in operations.tsp

decisions.decision: Currency is a discriminated union (envelope:none) rather than a flat polymorphic model
decisions.rationale: Enables SDK discriminator deserialization without a wrapper envelope field, matching the fiat/custom split at the type level.

decisions.decision: Models and operations split across currency.tsp and operations.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps model definitions free of HTTP concerns so they can be imported without pulling in HTTP decorator dependencies.
Currencies API is internal/unstable; stability decorators gate exposure in public-facing OpenAPI outputs and SDK generation.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new custom currency variant with create operation

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer API: core customer CRUD, billing data, Stripe checkout/portal sessions, credits sub-API, and charges sub-API. Root folder coordinates sub-domain imports via index.tsp.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for mutable entities
patterns.description: Core entities (Customer) spread ...Shared.Resource to inherit id, name, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at, and metadata fields consistently.

patterns.name: @visibility on every field with all applicable lifecycles
patterns.description: Each field explicitly lists all of Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Update that apply. Omitting Update for mutable fields causes them to be excluded from upsert payloads.

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on every model, enum, union
patterns.description: All exported TypeSpec types must have @friendlyName prefixed with 'Billing' to stabilize generated SDK names.

patterns.name: Shared.CreateRequest/UpsertRequest/CreateResponse/GetResponse/UpsertResponse wrappers
patterns.description: Operations use Shared generic request/response wrappers rather than bare model types or ad-hoc response shapes.

patterns.name: deepObject filter + PagePaginationQuery for list operations
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery and accept filter via @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }).
patterns.example: list(...Common.PagePaginationQuery, @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) filter?: ListCustomersParamsFilter)

patterns.name: Models in domain .tsp, HTTP ops in operations.tsp
patterns.description: customer.tsp and billing.tsp define models only. operations.tsp imports @typespec/http and declares all interface operations.
patterns.example: customer.tsp has no HTTP imports; operations.tsp: using TypeSpec.Http; interface CustomersOperations { @post create(...) }

patterns.name: Sub-domains as child folders imported via index.tsp
patterns.description: credits/ and charges/ are imported in index.tsp. New sub-domains follow the same pattern: add a folder with its own index.tsp and import it here.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Customer, Address, CustomerUsageAttribution, CustomerReference, CustomerKeyReference, UsageAttributionSubjectKey. No HTTP imports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing @visibility on new fields will default to all lifecycles. Spreading Shared.Resource is mandatory for CRUD entities.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CustomerBillingData model referencing Billing.BillingProfileReference and Apps.AppCustomerData. Bridges customer and billing/apps namespaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Imports apps and billing index.tsp; adding new billing-related customer fields here keeps billing concerns co-located.

key_file_guides.role: Declares CustomersOperations (CRUD + list), CustomerBillingOperations (get/upsert billing, app-data, Stripe sessions), and related request models.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each operation needs @operationId and @summary. Stripe operations use @route sub-paths. Error responses must include Common.ErrorResponses.

key_file_guides.role: Root import file for the customers folder; pulls in customer.tsp, operations.tsp, credits/index.tsp, charges/index.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New sub-domain folders must be imported here or they are excluded from compilation.

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) in customer.tsp or billing.tsp — they belong only in operations.tsp
Omitting @visibility on fields — fields default to all lifecycle phases, leaking write-only or system fields into create/update payloads
Omitting @friendlyName on new models or enums — the generated SDK name will be unstable or collide
Using inline pagination params instead of ...Common.PagePaginationQuery — causes drift from the standard pagination contract
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`

decisions.decision: Customer billing and app data live in billing.tsp, not customer.tsp
decisions.rationale: Keeps core customer identity (customer.tsp) free of billing/app coupling; billing.tsp bridges namespaces explicitly so the dependency is traceable.

decisions.decision: credits/ and charges/ are child sub-folders imported via index.tsp
decisions.rationale: Maintains logical separation of sub-domains while keeping the TypeSpec compilation path unified through the parent index.

decisions.decision: Stripe-specific operations use @route sub-paths within CustomerBillingOperations
decisions.rationale: Co-locates Stripe session operations with the billing interface they logically belong to, avoiding a proliferation of top-level interfaces.
